page: Amazon Web Services d3en.8xlarge is a dense storage instance optimized for network and EBS performance. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 32 dedicated virtual CPUs, corresponding to 16 physical cores with hyper-threading, running at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz. This setup is supported by 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz. A key hardware feature is its massive local storage capacity of 223,680 GB on HDD media, complemented by a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates that the single-core CPU performance of this instance is weak, falling into the bottom 25% in stress-ng and Geekbench tests. However, multi-core CPU performance scales to average levels, as demonstrated by stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core scores. Memory bandwidth for both read and write operations remains in the average tier. The instance handles Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput at average levels, but exhibits poor performance for large-scale LLM inference tasks.

Given its massive local HDD storage capacity and average multi-core processing capabilities, the d3en.8xlarge offers high resource density for storage-heavy workloads. It is highly suitable for applications requiring vast sequential data access, such as data warehousing, log processing, large-scale file systems, and database backups. The primary trade-off involves lower single-threaded compute efficiency, making it less suitable for compute-bound or low-latency single-core applications.
description: Amazon Web Services d3en.8xlarge is a dense storage instance designed for network and EBS-optimized workloads. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL x86_64 processor, it provides 32 dedicated vCPUs, 128.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and a massive 223,680 GB of local HDD storage. The instance features a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks show weak single-core CPU capabilities, but average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance. It is highly suited for storage-intensive applications, large-scale data warehousing, and sequential log processing where massive local capacity is required, though it presents a tradeoff in single-threaded compute speed.
og_description: Amazon Web Services d3en.8xlarge is a dense storage instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, 223,680 GB of local HDD storage, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth for storage-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services d3en.8xlarge offers 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 223,680 GB of local HDD storage for dense storage and database workloads.
tagline: A dense storage instance combining massive local HDD capacity with high-speed networking for data-intensive and sequential workloads.
bullet_points: Equipped with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory
Features 223,680 GB of local HDD storage for massive data capacity
Demonstrates average multi-core performance and weak single-core performance
Best suited for data warehousing, log processing, and large-scale file systems
